title: pterocarpus cumingii
intro: Pterocarpus cumingii, also known as the Philippine ironwood, is a species of tree in the family Fabaceae. It is highly valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds. The plant's extracts have been used to treat various health issues, such as fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal problems, and it is also believed to have antioxidant and cardiovascular benefits. Pterocarpus cumingii is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and its wood is used for traditional medicine and construction due to its durability and resistance to rot.
